title: Green Loan Principles
methodology: Industry consensus document maintained jointly by LMA, APLMA, and LSTA.
contributions: Defines four pillars for green-loan governance: use of proceeds, process for project evaluation and selection, management of proceeds, and reporting.
Voluntary, market-led standard; widely adopted by syndicated-loan participants but not legally binding.
weaknesses: Voluntary; compliance is not externally audited.
Project-evaluation criteria are intentionally non-prescriptive, leaving wide variance across lenders.
Updated periodically; ensure citation uses the current (Feb 2023) version.
emerald_relevance: Authority on what 'green loan' means in practice. Useful framing in introduction and section 4.5. The 'use of proceeds' pillar is the closest thing to a definition we have for the dataset's labelling.
search_query: Green Loan Principles LMA APLMA LSTA 2023 PDF
notes: Voluntary governance standard; defines use-of-proceeds, evaluation, reporting.
